decisions.briefing: Markets are in risk-off mode today with NASDAQ down 1.3% and S&P 500 down 0.7%, and the screener returned zero qualifying candidates. Cash sits at $444.94 — below the $500 threshold for new positions even if candidates existed. Holding all six current positions and letting the auto stop-loss and profit-target rules do their work.

market_education.summary_en: Markets shifted into risk-off territory on May 12, 2026, with the NASDAQ falling 1.3% as investors rotated away from growth and technology names amid broader macro uncertainty. Risk-off moves are typically triggered by a combination of factors — rising bond yields, hawkish Fed signals, geopolitical flare-ups, or disappointing economic data — that make the reward-to-risk ratio on equities less attractive. The broad-based selling across indices suggests institutional players are reducing exposure rather than targeting a single sector.
market_education.summary_zh: 2026年5月12日，美股全面轉入避險模式，那斯達克指數領跌1.3%，標普500亦下挫0.7%，投資者紛紛撤出科技及高成長類股。避險情緒升溫的背後，通常牽涉多重因素的疊加——包括美債殖利率走高、聯準會偏鷹言論、地緣政治風險升溫，或是不如預期的經濟數據——使股市的風險報酬比顯得不再吸引。指數層面的廣泛賣壓顯示，此次並非個別行業輪動，而是機構投資者整體降低風險敞口的主動操作。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: A 'risk-off' environment is when investors broadly sell higher-risk assets like growth stocks and move into safer havens such as government bonds, gold, or cash. It signals that market participants are more focused on protecting capital than chasing returns. On risk-off days, even fundamentally solid stocks can drop simply because the prevailing mood is fearful or uncertain.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「避險（Risk-Off）」是指市場參與者普遍將資金從股票等風險資產撤出，轉入國債、黃金或現金等安全資產的市場狀態。這種情緒反映投資者的首要目標已從追求收益轉為保全本金。在避險交易日，即便基本面穩健的股票也可能因市場整體情緒悲觀而遭到拖累。
